metadata.title:
GETS THE


BRIDGE JOB

NEW YORK EXPERT TO PASS


HIS OPINION ON MONROE ST.
STRUCTURE AND POSSIBLY
DRAW PLANS FOR NEW ONE.

pages.raw_text:
The city council last night, in
special committee. authorized the
employment of William H. Burr, OF
New York, as bridge expert, to
pass on the Monroe st. bridge and
recommend new structure either
steel or concrete, as he though best
suited for the locality and
geographical conditions. The other
expert under consideration was J..
A. L. Waddell, of Kansas City. On
account of his being altogether a
steel expert, and many of the
councilmen leaning toward the con-
crete style of structure, Waddell
was given little consideration.
Waddell was highly favored by the
Washington Water Power Co.,
with which the council is having
so much trouble over an agree
ment as to its share of the expense
of new bridge. He is already ia
miliar with every piece of steel
and bolt in the bridge, having been
sent plans by the w. w. P.


Mr.. Burr offers to expert the
bridge for $1,000 and transporta-
ton both ways from New York.
Waddell's proposition was $500,
without transportation. If it is de-
cided to have Burr make the plans,
then he will be paid 31a per cent
Of the cost of the construction,
which would give him almost $12.-
600 for his work, according to the
100000 estimate of the engineer
ing department of the city.


A letter was mailed to the expert
today asking him to get on the
ground and begin work as soon as
pOSSihlo
